channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: Operation Epic Confusion
item.description: Trump’s goals in Iran are expansive, shifting, and potentially impossible.

item.title: How the Gulf Countries Are Responding to Iran’s Attacks
item.description: Abu Dhabi, Doha, and Riyadh are weighing their options.

item.title: The Iran War Spills Beyond the Middle East
item.description: From Azerbaijan to the Indian Ocean, the conflict’s impact is spreading.

item.title: How the Iran War Might Impact Russia-Ukraine
item.description: The Middle Eastern conflict could drain Ukraine’s supply of key defensive munitions and push U.S. President Donald Trump to quickly reach a deal with Russia.

item.title: The Domino Effect of Operation Epic Fury
item.description: The U.S. war on Iran will weaken Islamist groups and embolden the Israeli right.

item.title: A Dangerous Opportunity for Iran’s Kurds
item.description: Only by working together can Kurdish groups and the Iranian opposition forge a better future.

item.title: The United States Could Lose the Gulf
item.description: Iran’s attacks on its neighbors are a reminder that the United States cannot protect them.

item.title: The Reductive Rhetoric of the Iran War
item.description: Americans across the political spectrum need to stop talking about Tehran in cliches.

item.title: Beijing’s Balkan Classrooms
item.description: While Confucius Institutes are closing across parts of Western Europe, China has found a more welcoming political environment in the Balkans.

item.title: The Drone Attrition Trap
item.description: The Middle East conflict shows Washington hasn’t yet learned enough about cheap drones from Kyiv.

item.title: Why Aren’t Energy Markets Reacting More to the Iran War?
item.description: Each day of war that passes adds to the physical risks to global oil and gas, but so far markets have basically shrugged.

item.title: The First 36 Hours of War Consumed Over 3,000 U.S.-Israeli Munitions
item.description: Replenishing stockpiles depends on vulnerable critical mineral chains.

item.title: Breaking Up Iran Would Be Catastrophic
item.description: It may suit Israel to foment separatist movements in Iran, but that doesn’t mean it serves the interests of the United States or its NATO allies. Western powers have a very poor track record with such strategies, and that would not change with a Balkanized Iran, which would produce one crisis after another.

item.title: Umar Khalid: 2,000 Days in Prison Without Trial
item.description: His continued incarceration is a telling example of the erosion of both India’s judicial independence and its democracy.

item.title: America’s Attack on Iran Should Have Asia-Pacific Countries Very, Very Worried
item.description: Having overthrown regimes in Latin America and the Middle East, why would Washington draw the line at Asia?

item.title: Assessing North Korea’s New Economic Five-Year Plan
item.description: From energy to innovation, Kim Jong Un’s growth strategy is up against structural limits.

item.title: A Partnership of Convenience: Why China Isn’t Intervening On Iran’s Behalf
item.description: The intense U.S. bombing of Iran reveals the limits of Beijing’s relationship with Tehran and the pragmatism of China’s Middle Eastern strategy

item.title: The Iran War Is Upending Global Energy Markets
item.description: The longer the US-Israeli campaign against Iran lasts, the greater the implications for the global energy system. Whereas short conflicts produce volatility, prolonged instability reshapes everything from trade flows to assessments of infrastructure risk and long-term investment behavior.

item.title: ‘Strategic Cousins’ Australia and Canada Chart a Closer Course
item.description: Both countries are navigating a geopolitical environment far more turbulent than the one that shaped their post-Cold War outlook.

item.title: Why Is Afghanistan Developing a Drone Industry?
item.description: Air and air-defense capabilities remain Afghanistan’s most vulnerable security component.

item.title: The 5 Best Books for Understanding Iran Today
item.description: Reads that shed light on the origins of today’s conflict—and Tehran’s possible future.

item.title: What Makes America Strong?
item.description: In 2025, US net migration was likely negative, as the number of legal immigrants has been reduced in various ways. The overall effect will be to slow economic growth – perhaps by a lot – relative to what it otherwise could be.

item.title: Is India Torpedoing Its Claims to Being a Net Security Provider in the IOR?
item.description: The U.S. sank an Iranian warship, which was returning home from an India-hosted exercise, off the Sri Lankan coast yesterday. India’s silence in response is deafening.

item.title: The Birth of European Nuclear Deterrence
item.description: With French President Emmanuel Macron's announcement of a new doctrine of "forward deterrence," Europe is finally building a nuclear strategy that fully belongs to Europeans. The age of strategic dependence on the United States is drawing to a close.

item.title: On Iran, China Cares About the Region More Than the Regime
item.description: Beijing won’t risk its broader Middle East interests to save the Islamic Republic.

item.title: Strengthening Women’s Rights Organizations Drives Economic Gains
item.description: Following foreign-aid cuts in donor countries, many developing countries have implemented austerity measures, to the detriment of women’s jobs, services, and protections. But donors and policymakers can deliver shared gains by investing in women’s rights organizations and embracing gender-based budgeting.

item.title: Viktor Orban Is Going Down Swinging
item.description: Hungary’s populist prime minister is in a fight for his political life—and acting like it.

item.title: How the US-Iran War Has Upended Pakistan’s Diplomacy
item.description: Saudi Arabia, wary of further escalation, is unlikely to press Pakistan to deploy troops or join the conflict at this stage.

item.title: Operation Epic Fury Moves East: The Iran Conflict Has Left the Middle East
item.description: The possibility of expansion, into Asia and elsewhere, is no longer theoretical.

item.title: The Coming Showdown Over Cuba
item.description: How escalating U.S. pressure could reshape the island.

item.title: Why China Won’t Help Iran
item.description: Beijing cares about the oil, not the regime.

item.title: The Abiding Question of the Iranian Bomb
item.description: America needs a plan for Tehran's nuclear program.

item.title: Board of Peace Talks ‘On Hold’ Due to Iran Conflict, Indonesia Says
item.description: Jakarta’s participation in U.S. President Donald Trump’s initiative has drawn widespread criticism, particularly from conservative Islamic groups.

item.title: In Photos: The Border Conflict Between Thailand and Cambodia
item.description: Scenes from the political and military front lines of last year's undeclared war.

item.title: ASEAN Calls For ‘Self-restraint’, Return to Diplomacy as Iran War Intensifies
item.description: The flat tone of the bloc's statements belies the severe disruptions that the war could have on Southeast Asia's export-dependent economies.

item.title: The US Hasn’t Designated Violators of International Religious Freedom Since 2023
item.description: In its latest report, USCIRF has urged the Trump administration to finally make designations of egregious violators of religious freedoms.
